     The Numbers Business Report

King Arthur: Legend of the Sword (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $39,175,066Details
International Box Office $110,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $149,175,066
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,548,287
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,834,276
Total $17,382,563
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When the child Arthur’s father is murdered, Vortigern, Arthur’s uncle, seizes the crown. Robbed of his birthright and with no idea who he truly is, Arthur comes up the hard way in the back alleys of the city. But once he pulls the sword from the stone, his life is turned upside down and he is forced to acknowledge his true legacy…whether he likes it or not.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$15,371,270 (39.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.55 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:26.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,702 opening theaters/3,702 max. theaters, 3.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $52,233,422

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 12th, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: April 21st, 2017 (Special Engagement) (South Korea)
May 11th, 2017 (Wide), released as Крал Артур: Легенда за меча (Bulgaria)
May 11th, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
May 12th, 2017 (Wide) (Slovakia)
May 12th, 2017 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: August 8th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of violence and action, some suggestive content and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2466 (Cert #50330), 3/8/2017)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Footage Shown in Reverse
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion
Plot point: Orphan, Prophecy, Revenge, Royalty, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Sword & Sorcerer
Subgenre: Heroic Adventure
Time period setting: Middle Ages
Source:Based on Folk Tale/Legend/Fairytale
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Village Roadshow Productions, Weed Road Pictures, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment, Safehouse Pictures
Production Countries: United States, Australia
Languages: English

When the child Arthur’s father is murdered, Vortigern, Arthur’s uncle, seizes the crown. Robbed of his birthright and with no idea who he truly is, Arthur comes up the hard way in the back alleys of the city. But once he pulls the sword from the stone, his life is turned upside down and he is forced to acknowledge his true legacy…whether he likes it or not.
